title: 10 Things I love about Stars of Akarios: A Great Game that's Not for Me
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WvVMuiXrqcI

After playing the prologue, Daniel (@dungeondive) delivers 'ten things I love' about Stars of Akarios — and the one reason he's still going to sell it. He pushes back on the 'Gloomhaven in space' label, arguing it plays more like a super-gamebook fused with a board game, thanks to a scenario book thick with choices, branching paths and side missions. His ten highlights: almost no chits (statuses tracked by cubes on the boards); superbly designed player boards holding everything you need; genuinely fast setup and teardown; a modifier deck that adds ability cards (repair, resupply, hack) and rollover draws, not just plus-ones; a fail-forward system that eases losing streaks; dice-allocation actions; stress spent as a push-your-luck currency with trauma consequences; quick, chess-like space combat where completing manoeuvres matters; painless solo scaling (five dice instead of four); and a footprint that fits his small table. The dealbreaker is simply the campaign time commitment — he knows a boxed campaign won't come back off the shelf, and his channel data shows series videos bleed views. He dreams of a condensed zero-to-hero mode you could finish in three hours, and rates the writing merely okay. High praise, wrong game for him.

----

Daniel loves nearly everything about Stars of Akarios but can't commit to a long campaign — and even cites his view stats. Do you finish the big campaign games you buy, or do they quietly gather dust?
